turns.content: ok I know its may and this is insane but me and the boys already started talkin about our fantasy football draft and I need a strategy
May is early, but honestly the people who start thinking about it now are usually the ones who win their leagues. What kind of league are you in — standard scoring, PPR, half PPR?
half ppr 10 team league. I had the 6th pick last year and went rb heavy and it was a disaster bro I finished like 8th it was embarrassin

8th in a 10-team league stings, especially if you went in with a plan. Going RB heavy can work, but it depends a lot on which RBs you land. What went wrong — injuries, guys underperforming, or did you neglect other positions?

injuries killed me dude. my rb1 went down like week 4 and then I was scramblin on waivers all season. plus I waited way too long on quarterback and got stuck with like the 15th best qb which is brutal

That's the classic downside of RB heavy — running backs have the highest injury rate of any position, so you're exposed to a lot of risk. And waiting too long on QB in a 10-team league is rough because the drop-off from the top tier to the mid tier is significant. There just aren't enough elite QBs to go around.
exactly so this year im thinkin about maybe goin like elite qb or elite tight end early and then loadin up on wide receivers in the middle rounds cause the wr pool is so deep

That's a smart pivot. In half PPR, wide receivers are incredibly deep — you can find solid WR2 and flex options in rounds 5-8 that will put up consistent numbers. Grabbing an elite TE like Kelce early gives you a massive positional advantage every week because the drop-off at tight end is the steepest of any position. Or if a top-3 QB falls to you, that weekly floor is hard to beat. The key is don't reach — take the best available value and let the board come to you.

yeah thats what I keep hearin let the board come to you but then I get in the draft and panic and take a guy I like instead of the smart pick lol. like last year I took a patriots rb in the 4th cause im a homer and that was so dumb

Fantasy football and fandom have to stay separate — that's rule number one. Drafting a Patriot because you love the team is how you end up in 8th place. This year, make a tier list ahead of time so when you're on the clock you're just picking the highest-ranked name on your board instead of going with your gut. Takes the emotion out of it.
a tier list thats actually smart. like rank guys in groups so I know whos in the same range and just grab whoever falls to me from the top group

Exactly. If you have a tier of four wide receivers you like equally, you don't need to reach for one in round 4 — wait and grab whichever one is still there in round 5 or 6. The tier system also helps you identify value picks when everyone else is reaching. If guys from your tier 2 are still on the board when you expected them to be gone, that's when you pounce.

dude where was this advice last year I literally just went in with a list of guys I thought were cool and hoped for the best. no wonder I got smoked. im definitely doin the tier thing on a spreadsheet or somethin so I can just check it off durin the draft

A spreadsheet is the way to go. Color code your tiers so you can see at a glance where the value is during the draft. Cross off names as they get picked and you'll always know who the best available player is at every position. It's a simple system but it works.
ok bet im gonna start workin on that this summer. the boys are gonna be shook when I actually have a plan this year instead of just wingin it. appreciate it dude
Preparation is the difference between finishing 8th and making the playoffs. Start building those tiers once preseason rankings come out and you'll be in good shape. Good luck this year.
